seoTitle: Unisplendour Products | FPGA, Smart Card ICs, Power Management | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Explore Unisplendour comprehensive product portfolio including FPGA devices, smart card ICs, power management chips, and industrial control solutions. Authorized distributor with technical support.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by Unisplendour?
faqs.answer: Unisplendour offers four core product categories: FPGA Products ranging from CPLD to high-performance SoC FPGA devices for communications and industrial applications, Smart Card ICs including secure microcontrollers and contact/contactless chips for banking and telecommunications, Power Management ICs featuring DC-DC converters, LDO regulators, and battery management solutions, and Industrial Control ICs for motor control, power metering, and automation applications. Each category is designed to provide reliable, high-performance semiconductor solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories to find the right Unisplendour components for your application. Contact our FAE team for personalized product recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Unisplendour FPGA for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Unisplendour FPGA involves analyzing your logic capacity requirements, I/O needs, power budget, and cost constraints. For simple glue logic and small designs, Compact series CPLD devices offer cost-effective solutions. For medium-complexity designs requiring 10K-100K logic elements, Logos series FPGAs provide optimal performance-cost balance. For high-performance applications requiring 100K+ logic elements, DSP blocks, or high-speed transceivers, Titan series SoC FPGAs are recommended. Consider future expansion needs and development tool requirements when making your selection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our product selection guides or contact our FAE engineers for expert assistance in choosing the optimal Unisplendour FPGA.

faqs.question: What support does BeiLuo provide for Unisplendour products?
faqs.answer: As an authorized Unisplendour distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive support including technical consultation for FPGA design and verification, product selection assistance based on application requirements, reference design review for power management and control applications, smart card personalization and initialization support, programming and testing services, supply chain management and inventory planning, competitive pricing and volume discounts, fast delivery from local stock. Our FAE team has deep expertise in Unisplendour product portfolio and application development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical support team for immediate assistance with your Unisplendour product inquiries and design challenges.

faqs.question: Which Unisplendour products are best suited for telecommunications applications?
faqs.answer: Unisplendour products are widely used in telecommunications infrastructure. The Titan series FPGAs are ideal for 5G baseband processing, fronthaul interfaces, and optical transport networks due to their high-speed transceivers and DSP capabilities. Logos series FPGAs suit protocol bridging and interface conversion applications. For power management in telecom equipment, Unisplendour's DC-DC converters and LDO regulators provide efficient power solutions. Smart card ICs can be used for secure authentication in network equipment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Filter products by telecom application requirements or contact our communications FAE team for recommendations on optimal solutions.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Unisplendour FPGA series?
faqs.answer: Unisplendour offers three main FPGA series: Compact series are CPLD devices with 32-512 macrocells for simple logic and glue logic applications. Logos series are mid-range FPGAs with 10K-100K logic elements, suitable for industrial control, communications, and consumer electronics. Titan series are high-performance SoC FPGAs with 100K+ logic elements, embedded processors, high-speed transceivers, and DSP blocks for demanding applications like 5G base stations and high-performance computing. Choose based on your complexity, performance, and cost requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Compact for simple logic, Logos for general applications, Titan for high-performance requirements.

categories.name: FPGA Products
categories.description: Unisplendour FPGA portfolio includes CPLD, low-cost FPGA, and high-performance SoC FPGA devices for communications, industrial, and automotive applications.
categories.longDescription: As an authorized Unisplendour distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive selection and support for Unisplendour's FPGA portfolio. The Compact series CPLD devices offer cost-effective solutions for simple logic and glue logic applications. The Logos series FPGAs provide optimal performance-cost balance for industrial control, communications, and consumer electronics. The Titan series SoC FPGAs deliver high-performance capabilities with embedded processors, DSP blocks, and high-speed transceivers for demanding applications like 5G base stations and high-performance computing. Our FAE team can guide you through the selection process and provide design support.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Unisplendour FPGA
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for choosing the right FPGA for your application

faqs.question: How do I choose between CPLD and FPGA?
faqs.answer: Choose CPLD (Compact series) for simple combinational logic, glue logic, and small state machines with deterministic timing. CPLDs offer instant-on configuration, low power, and predictable delays. Choose FPGA (Logos/Titan series) for complex designs requiring 1000+ logic elements, DSP functions, memory blocks, or high-speed interfaces. FPGAs offer greater capacity, embedded functions, and flexibility but have more complex power-up configuration. For designs under 500 logic elements with simple requirements, CPLD is usually more cost-effective.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use CPLD for simple logic under 500 elements. Use FPGA for complex designs requiring DSP or memory.

faqs.question: What development tools are available for Unisplendour FPGA?
faqs.answer: Unisplendour provides the Pango Design Suite (PDS) for FPGA development. PDS includes design entry tools supporting VHDL, Verilog, and schematic capture, synthesis engine optimized for Unisplendour architectures, place and route with timing analysis, simulation integration with ModelSim, and programming and debugging tools. The software supports Windows and Linux platforms. IP cores library includes commonly used functions like PCIe, DDR controllers, and Ethernet MAC. Evaluation boards are available for major device families.
faqs.decisionGuide: Download Pango Design Suite from Unisplendour website. Contact BeiLuo for evaluation board recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I estimate resource requirements for my FPGA design?
faqs.answer: Estimate FPGA resources by analyzing your design: Count flip-flops for registers and state machines. Count LUTs for combinational logic (typically 4-6 LUTs per complex logic function). Add DSP blocks for multipliers and MAC operations. Calculate memory requirements for RAM and FIFOs. Add 20-30% margin for routing and future expansion. Pango Design Suite provides resource estimation during synthesis. Start with a larger device for prototyping, then optimize for production. BeiLuo FAEs can review your design requirements and recommend appropriate devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use synthesis tool estimates. Add 30% margin. Prototype with larger device, optimize for production.

faqs.question: What are the power requirements for Unisplendour FPGAs?
faqs.answer: Unisplendour FPGA power requirements vary by series and utilization: Compact CPLDs typically require 50-200mW. Logos FPGAs range from 0.5W to 3W depending on size and activity. Titan SoC FPGAs can require 5-15W for high-performance configurations. Power supplies needed: Core voltage (1.0V or 1.2V depending on device), I/O voltage (1.8V, 2.5V, 3.3V), and auxiliary voltage (2.5V or 3.3V). Pango Design Suite provides power estimation tools. Consider thermal management for devices dissipating >2W.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use PDS power estimator. Plan thermal management for >2W devices. Use multiple rails for clean power.

faqs.question: How do I program and configure Unisplendour FPGAs?
faqs.answer: Unisplendour FPGAs support multiple configuration modes: Active Serial (AS) from external SPI flash, Passive Serial (PS) from external processor, JTAG for debugging and programming, and internal configuration for SoC devices with embedded flash. Configuration bitstream is generated by Pango Design Suite. Use USB-Blaster or compatible programmer for JTAG. SPI flash can be programmed in-system or pre-programmed. SoC FPGAs can boot from internal flash then load larger bitstream from external memory.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SPI flash for standalone operation. Use JTAG for development. Use PS mode for processor-controlled configuration.

products.name: Titan Series High-Performance SoC FPGA
products.shortDescription: High-performance SoC FPGA with 180K logic elements, dual-core ARM, and 12.5Gbps transceivers for communications.
descriptionParagraphs: The PGT180H is Unisplendour's flagship SoC FPGA, combining programmable logic with dual-core ARM Cortex-A9 processors.
With 180,000 logic elements, 800 DSP blocks, and 16 high-speed transceivers supporting up to 12.5Gbps, it addresses demanding applications in 5G communications and high-performance computing.
The integrated hard processor system runs Linux and provides high-bandwidth connectivity to the programmable fabric through AXI interfaces.

faeReview.author: Dr. Wei Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - FPGA Applications
faeReview.content: The PGT180H represents Unisplendour's entry into the high-performance FPGA market, competing directly with Xilinx Zynq and Intel Arria. I've worked with customers deploying this device in 5G base stations and optical transport equipment. The integration of dual-core ARM processors with high-performance programmable fabric enables complete system-on-chip solutions. The 12.5Gbps transceivers handle CPRI and Ethernet protocols reliably. Power consumption is competitive with international alternatives. Development using Pango Design Suite has improved significantly - the tool flow is now mature enough for production designs. For customers looking for domestic FPGA solutions with high performance, the PGT180H is a compelling option.
faeReview.highlight: High-performance SoC FPGA with competitive features

alternativeParts.reason: Higher capacity alternative with established ecosystem
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring >200K logic elements or extensive ecosystem support

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost alternative for less demanding applications

companionParts.description: Comprehensive evaluation kit for PGT180H development
8GB DDR4 SDRAM for PGT180H memory interface

faqs.question: What is the maximum data rate for PGT180H transceivers?
faqs.answer: The PGT180H features 16 high-speed transceivers supporting data rates from 614 Mbps to 12.5 Gbps per channel. These transceivers support various protocols including PCIe Gen3, 10G Ethernet, CPRI, Interlaken, and SATA. The transceivers include programmable equalization and pre-emphasis to compensate for channel losses. For 5G fronthaul applications, the 9.8Gbps CPRI rate is fully supported. The transceivers are compatible with standard SFP+ and XFP optical modules.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify protocol requirements against transceiver specifications. Contact FAE for signal integrity analysis.

faqs.question: How does the ARM processor connect to the FPGA fabric?
faqs.answer: The dual-core ARM Cortex-A9 processors connect to the programmable fabric through multiple high-bandwidth AXI interfaces: AXI bus matrix provides non-blocking connectivity between processors and FPGA logic. Accelerator Coherency Port (ACP) allows FPGA logic to access processor cache. Multiple AXI master and slave ports support various data flow patterns. Maximum theoretical bandwidth exceeds 100 Gbps between processor and fabric. This architecture enables tight integration of software and hardware processing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ACP for cache-coherent data sharing. Use AXI masters for high-throughput data movement.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of PGT180H?
faqs.answer: PGT180H power consumption depends on utilization and activity: Static power (device idle): 2-3W. Typical dynamic power: 5-8W for moderate utilization. Maximum power (fully utilized): 15-20W. Power breakdown: Programmable logic (40-50%), Transceivers (30-40%), Processor system (10-20%). Pango Design Suite provides power estimation based on actual design. Thermal management required for designs >10W. Recommended: heatsink with airflow for typical applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use PDS power estimator. Plan thermal management for >10W designs.

faqs.question: What memory interfaces does PGT180H support?
faqs.answer: PGT180H supports various memory interfaces through hard and soft controllers: DDR3/DDR3L up to 1866 Mbps (hard controller). DDR4 up to 2400 Mbps (hard controller). LPDDR3 for low-power applications. QSPI flash for boot and storage. Parallel NOR flash. The hard memory controllers support up to 72-bit wide interfaces with ECC. Maximum memory bandwidth exceeds 25 GB/s with dual-channel DDR4. Soft controllers can be implemented in fabric for specialized memory types.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use hard DDR4 controller for maximum performance. Use QSPI for cost-sensitive boot storage.

faqs.question: What is the development flow for PGT180H?
faqs.answer: PGT180H development follows standard FPGA design flow: Design entry using VHDL, Verilog, or schematic in Pango Design Suite. Functional simulation using ModelSim or integrated simulator. Synthesis targeting PGT180H architecture. Place and route with timing optimization. Static timing analysis to verify performance. Bitstream generation for configuration. Programming via JTAG or SPI flash. For SoC designs, software development using ARM DS-5 or GCC toolchain. Linux BSP available for processor subsystem.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Pango Design Suite for complete flow. Contact BeiLuo for software licensing.

faqs.question: Is PGT180H suitable for 5G base station applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, PGT180H is well-suited for 5G base station applications: 12.5Gbps transceivers support eCPRI/CPRI front haul interfaces. Sufficient DSP blocks for massive MIMO precoding. Dual-core ARM processors for L2/L3 processing. High-speed memory interfaces for data buffering. PCIe Gen3 for accelerator cards. Multiple devices deployed in 5G small cell and macro base stations in China. Reference designs available for common 5G baseband functions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate against specific 3GPP requirements. Contact FAE for 5G reference designs.

products.name: Logos Series Mid-Range FPGA
products.shortDescription: Cost-effective mid-range FPGA with 50K logic elements, 120 DSP blocks, and high-speed I/O for industrial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The PGL50H is a mid-range FPGA in Unisplendour's Logos series, offering 50,000 logic elements and comprehensive peripheral support.
With 120 DSP blocks, 2.7 Mbit embedded memory, and high-speed I/O supporting up to 1.6Gbps, it addresses a wide range of industrial and communications applications.
The device provides an optimal balance of performance, features, and cost for medium-complexity designs.

faeReview.author: Li Ming
faeReview.title: FAE - Industrial Applications
faeReview.content: The PGL50H is my go-to recommendation for industrial customers needing FPGA capabilities without the cost of high-end devices. I've deployed this in numerous PLC systems, motor controllers, and communications gateways. The 50K logic elements handle most industrial control algorithms comfortably, and the 120 DSP blocks are sufficient for multi-axis motor control. The high-speed I/O supports Gigabit Ethernet and industrial protocols like EtherCAT. Power consumption is reasonable - typically 2-3W in active operation. The price point is very competitive compared to similar capacity devices from Xilinx and Intel. For customers transitioning from international suppliers, the PGL50H offers a smooth migration path.

alternativeParts.reason: Alternative with established Cyclone IV ecosystem
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring Intel toolchain or specific IP cores

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost alternative for smaller designs

companionParts.description: Evaluation kit with comprehensive peripheral interfaces
Gigabit Ethernet PHY for industrial networking

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption of PGL50H?
faqs.answer: PGL50H power consumption varies with utilization: Static power: 150-250mW. Typical dynamic power: 1-2W for moderate designs. Maximum power: 3-4W for fully utilized devices. Power scales linearly with logic utilization and clock frequency. The device supports multiple power-down modes for power-sensitive applications. No heatsink required for most applications. Thermal analysis recommended for designs >2.5W.
faqs.decisionGuide: Estimate power using PDS tool. No heatsink needed for typical industrial applications.

faqs.question: Does PGL50H support Gigabit Ethernet?
faqs.answer: PGL50H supports Gigabit Ethernet through soft IP cores and external PHY: GMII/RGMII interface to external Gigabit PHY. Soft Ethernet MAC IP available from Unisplendour. TCP/IP stack runs in embedded processor or soft core. Typical throughput: 800-900 Mbps with soft MAC. For higher performance, consider Titan series with hard MAC. Multiple Ethernet ports possible depending on I/O availability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use external PHY with RGMII interface. Evaluate throughput requirements for soft vs hard MAC.

faqs.question: What industrial protocols can PGL50H implement?
faqs.answer: PGL50H can implement various industrial communication protocols: EtherCAT slave controller (soft IP). PROFINET IO device. Modbus TCP/RTU master and slave. CAN bus controller with external transceiver. Ethernet/IP adapter. CC-Link IE Field. These protocols are implemented using soft cores in the programmable fabric. Reference designs and IP cores available from Unisplendour and third parties. I/O count and speed requirements determine number of concurrent protocols.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check IP core availability for specific protocols. Contact FAE for reference designs.

faqs.question: How many motor control axes can PGL50H support?
faqs.answer: PGL50H can support multiple motor control axes depending on complexity: Simple stepper control: 8-16 axes. Vector control (FOC) for PMSM/BLDC: 4-6 axes. Servo control with advanced algorithms: 2-4 axes. Each axis typically requires: 2-4K logic elements, 2-4 DSP blocks for PID and transforms, encoder interface logic. Reference designs available for 3-phase motor control. External gate drivers and power stage required.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate based on control algorithm complexity. Contact FAE for motor control reference designs.

faqs.question: What configuration options does PGL50H support?
faqs.answer: PGL50H supports multiple configuration modes: Active Serial (AS) from SPI flash - most common. Passive Serial (PS) from external processor. JTAG for debugging and programming. Configuration time: 50-200ms from SPI flash depending on bitstream size. Supports configuration decompression to reduce flash size. Multiple configuration images possible for fail-safe boot. Remote update capability through embedded processor.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SPI flash for standalone operation. Use JTAG for development. Implement dual-image for critical applications.

faqs.question: Is PGL50H suitable for functional safety applications?
faqs.answer: PGL50H can be used in functional safety applications with appropriate design: Implement dual-channel architecture in fabric. Use external safety monitor circuits. Diagnostic coverage through built-in self-test. Temperature and voltage monitoring. Note: PGL50H is not certified for specific SIL levels. Safety certification requires system-level design and validation. Contact Unisplendour for safety manual and failure mode analysis.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement redundancy at system level. Contact Unisplendour for safety documentation.

categories.name: Smart Card ICs
categories.description: Secure microcontrollers and contact/contactless smart card chips for banking, telecommunications, identity, and IoT security applications.
categories.longDescription: As an authorized Unisplendour distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive selection and support for smart card ICs. Unisplendour's portfolio provides industry-leading security for banking, telecommunications, and identity applications. The THD series secure microcontrollers are certified to Common Criteria EAL5+, meeting the highest security standards for financial transactions. These chips support multiple interfaces including contact (ISO 7816) and contactless (ISO 14443) operation, enabling versatile deployment in cards, mobile devices, and IoT applications. With comprehensive Java Card and native OS support, developers can implement secure applications for payment, authentication, and data protection. BeiLuo provides secure provisioning services and technical support for smart card personalization.

selectionGuide.title: Smart Card IC Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting secure microcontrollers for payment and identity applications

faqs.question: What security certifications do Unisplendour smart card ICs have?
faqs.answer: Unisplendour smart card ICs hold Common Criteria EAL5+ certification, the highest commercially available security rating. This certification covers the entire product lifecycle including design, manufacturing, and testing. The chips also comply with EMVCo specifications for payment applications, FIPS 140-2 Level 3 for cryptographic modules, and PCI PTS for point-of-sale terminals. These certifications ensure the products meet stringent security requirements for financial and government applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify specific certification requirements for your application. Contact FAE for certification documentation.

faqs.question: What is the difference between contact and contactless smart cards?
faqs.answer: Contact smart cards use physical electrical connections through gold-plated pads (ISO 7816 interface). They offer high security and are standard for banking cards. Contactless smart cards use RF communication (ISO 14443) and are accessed by tapping or waving near a reader. They offer convenience and faster transactions. Dual-interface cards support both modes. Contact cards typically have higher security while contactless offers better user experience. Unisplendour provides chips for all three types.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use contact for maximum security applications. Use contactless for convenience and speed. Dual for flexibility.

faqs.question: How do I develop applications for Unisplendour smart card ICs?
faqs.answer: Unisplendour smart card ICs support multiple development environments: Java Card platform for portable applet development, Native C/Assembly for maximum performance, GlobalPlatform for secure application management. Development tools include: Unisplendour Development Kit with SDK, Eclipse-based IDE with debugger, Emulator for testing without physical cards, Personalization tools for production. Reference applets available for payment, identity, and secure storage applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Java Card for rapid development. Use native for performance-critical applications. Contact BeiLuo for dev kit.

faqs.question: What is the typical personalization process for smart cards?
faqs.answer: Smart card personalization involves multiple steps: Initialization - loading operating system and basic data, Pre-personalization - loading application frameworks, Personalization - writing cardholder data and keys, Quality assurance - testing functionality and security. Personalization requires secure facilities with controlled access. BeiLuo offers personalization services with certified secure infrastructure. Alternatively, customers can establish their own personalization bureau with Unisplendour's equipment and software.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use BeiLuo personalization services for small volumes. Establish own bureau for high volumes.

faqs.question: How do Unisplendour smart card ICs compare to NXP and Infineon?
faqs.answer: Unisplendour smart card ICs offer competitive features: Equivalent EAL5+ security certification, Comparable performance and memory options, Lower cost for high-volume deployments, Strong local support in China and Asia, Customization services for specific requirements. The main differences are ecosystem maturity and international brand recognition. For domestic Chinese deployments, Unisplendour offers supply chain advantages and regulatory compliance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate based on volume, geography, and ecosystem requirements. Contact FAE for detailed comparison.

products.name: High-End Secure Microcontroller
products.shortDescription: EAL5+ certified secure MCU with 400KB flash, dual-interface support for banking and high-security applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The THD89 is Unisplendour's flagship secure microcontroller, certified to Common Criteria EAL5+ for high-security applications.
With 400KB flash memory, 16KB RAM, and dual-interface (contact/contactless) support, it addresses demanding banking and identity applications.
The chip features advanced security mechanisms including side-channel attack protection, fault injection resistance, and secure cryptographic coprocessors.

faeReview.author: Dr. Chen Wei
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Security Products
faeReview.content: The THD89 is our top-tier security product, competing with NXP's SmartMX and Infineon's SLE97 families. I've supported multiple banking card deployments using this chip. The EAL5+ certification is genuine and recognized internationally. Performance is excellent - transaction times under 200ms for EMV contactless payments. The dual-interface flexibility allows issuers to deploy single product for both contact and contactless use cases. Security features are comprehensive including DPA/SPA countermeasures and active shielding. For customers looking for domestic secure microcontroller alternatives, THD89 is a mature, proven solution.
faeReview.highlight: EAL5+ certified with proven banking deployments

alternativeParts.reason: Established ecosystem with extensive banking references
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring maximum ecosystem support and international brand

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost alternative for single-interface applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Cost-sensitive applications not requiring dual interface

companionParts.description: Development kit with sample cards and SDK
USB contact card reader for development
USB contactless card reader for development

faqs.question: What cryptographic algorithms does THD89 support?
faqs.answer: THD89 supports comprehensive cryptographic functions: Symmetric: AES-128/192/256, DES, 3DES, SM4. Asymmetric: RSA up to 4096-bit, ECC (P-256, P-384, SM2). Hash functions: SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, SM3. All algorithms implemented in hardware with side-channel countermeasures. Performance: RSA-2048 signature in <100ms, AES encryption at >10MB/s. Chinese commercial cryptography (SM2/3/4) supported for domestic compliance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify specific algorithm requirements. All standard algorithms supported with high performance.

faqs.question: How is the EAL5+ certification validated?
faqs.answer: Common Criteria EAL5+ certification involves rigorous evaluation: Security target document defining security features. Formal design documentation and modeling. Vulnerability analysis including side-channel and fault attacks. Independent testing by certified evaluation lab. Manufacturing audit for secure production. Certificate issued by national certification body. THD89 certified by China ITSEC and recognized internationally through CCRA agreement. Validity can be verified on certification body website.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify certificate validity on official certification body website. Contact Unisplendour for certificate copy.

faqs.question: What is the transaction performance for EMV payments?
faqs.answer: THD89 EMV transaction performance: Contact transaction: 300-500ms typical. Contactless transaction: 150-250ms typical. Performance depends on: Cryptographic operations required, Application complexity, Terminal implementation, Communication speed. Meets all EMVCo timing requirements. Faster than many competitive products due to optimized cryptographic hardware. Benchmarks available from Unisplendour for specific use cases.
faqs.decisionGuide: Performance meets EMV requirements. Contact FAE for specific application benchmarks.

faqs.question: Does THD89 support Java Card?
faqs.answer: Yes, THD89 supports Java Card 3.0.1 Classic and Connected editions. Java Card Virtual Machine optimized for performance. GlobalPlatform 2.3.1 for secure application management. Supports multiple applications with firewall isolation. Development using standard Java Card tools. Migration path from other Java Card platforms available. Performance comparable to NXP and Oracle Java Card implementations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Java Card for portable applications. Use native for maximum performance.

faqs.question: What personalization services does BeiLuo offer?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo provides comprehensive personalization services: Secure facility with controlled access and monitoring. Key injection using certified hardware security modules. Data preparation and validation. Card printing and embossing. Quality assurance testing. Secure packaging and logistics. Small volume prototyping to high-volume production. Personalization bureau can be established at customer site for very high volumes. All services comply with payment industry security standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use BeiLuo services for prototyping and medium volumes. Establish own bureau for >1M cards/year.

faqs.question: What is the supply chain security for THD89?
faqs.answer: THD89 supply chain security includes: Secure manufacturing in certified facilities. Each chip has unique identifier traceable to wafer. Secure packaging with tamper evidence. Delivery through authorized distributors only. Certificate of authenticity provided. No gray market or counterfeit products when purchased through authorized channels. BeiLuo maintains secure inventory and provides full traceability documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Purchase only through authorized distributors. Verify authenticity certificates. Maintain traceability records.

products.name: Cost-Optimized Secure MCU
products.shortDescription: Cost-effective secure MCU with 80KB flash and contact interface for loyalty cards and basic security.
descriptionParagraphs: The THD20 is a cost-optimized secure microcontroller designed for high-volume applications requiring basic security features.
With 80KB flash memory and contact-only interface, it targets loyalty cards, prepaid cards, and entry-level security applications.
Despite the cost focus, the chip maintains essential security features including secure memory and cryptographic acceleration.

faeReview.author: Wang Hua
faeReview.title: FAE - Smart Card Applications
faeReview.content: The THD20 is our volume product for cost-sensitive smart card applications. I've supported deployments in loyalty programs, campus cards, and transportation systems with millions of units shipped. The cost is very competitive - often 30-40% lower than comparable NXP or Infineon products. Security is appropriate for the target applications with EAL4+ certification. While not suitable for banking, it's perfect for applications where basic security and low cost are priorities. The contact-only interface keeps costs down while supporting most card reader infrastructure.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective solution for high-volume card applications

alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring contactless operation and speed

alternativeParts.reason: Higher security and capacity for demanding applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring EAL5+ or contactless capability

faqs.question: What is the difference between THD20 and THD89?
faqs.answer: THD20 is cost-optimized with 80KB flash, EAL4+ security, and contact-only interface. THD89 is high-security with 400KB flash, EAL5+ certification, and dual interface. THD20 targets loyalty, prepaid, and basic access control. THD89 targets banking, national ID, and high-security applications. Price difference is significant - THD20 is 60-70% lower cost. Choose based on security requirements and application needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use THD20 for basic security and cost sensitivity. Use THD89 for high-security requirements.

faqs.question: Is THD20 suitable for payment applications?
faqs.answer: THD20 is NOT suitable for banking payment applications requiring EMV certification. EAL4+ security is insufficient for financial transactions. Limited cryptographic capabilities compared to banking requirements. Suitable for: Closed-loop prepaid systems, Loyalty point systems, Campus cards, Gift cards. For open-loop payment, use THD89 or THD86 with EAL5+ certification.
faqs.decisionGuide: Do NOT use for banking payment. Suitable for closed-loop prepaid and loyalty.

faqs.question: What is the typical card lifetime with THD20?
faqs.answer: THD20 EEPROM endurance supports: 500,000 write cycles per page. 10+ year data retention. Typical card lifetime: 3-5 years for daily-use cards. 5-10 years for occasional-use cards. End-of-life warning mechanism available. Memory management should distribute writes evenly. For longer lifetime requirements, consider THD86 with higher endurance specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for 3-5 year typical card life. Use THD86 for longer lifetime requirements.

faqs.question: Can THD20 be used for access control?
faqs.answer: Yes, THD20 is suitable for many access control applications: Employee badge systems with secure authentication. Visitor management with time-limited access. Parking system access cards. Gym and club membership cards. Physical access with secure challenge-response. For high-security facilities (government, data centers), consider THD89 with higher security certification.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for commercial access control. Use THD89 for high-security government applications.

faqs.question: What development tools are available for THD20?
faqs.answer: THD20 development uses: Unisplendour Smart Card SDK with C compiler and debugger. Eclipse-based IDE for code development. Card emulation and testing tools. Sample applications for common use cases. Personalization tools for production. Documentation includes user manual, programming guide, and security guidelines. Training available from BeiLuo for development teams.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact BeiLuo for SDK and development kit. Training available for new teams.

faqs.question: What is the minimum order quantity for THD20?
faqs.answer: THD20 is optimized for high-volume production: Minimum order: 10,000 units for standard products. Lower MOQ available for development and pilot projects. Volume pricing tiers: 100K, 500K, 1M+ units. Delivery: 4-6 weeks for standard orders. Faster delivery available from BeiLuo inventory for smaller quantities. Contact sales for specific pricing and delivery schedules.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for 10K+ MOQ for production. Use BeiLuo inventory for smaller development quantities.

categories.name: Power Management ICs
categories.description: High-efficiency power management solutions including DC-DC converters, LDO regulators, and battery management ICs for consumer and industrial applications.
categories.longDescription: As an authorized Unisplendour distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive selection and support for power management ICs. Unisplendour's portfolio delivers efficient power conversion solutions for a wide range of applications. The product line includes synchronous and asynchronous DC-DC converters with high efficiency across load ranges, low-dropout (LDO) regulators with excellent line and load regulation, and battery management ICs for lithium-ion and lithium-polymer batteries. These products serve consumer electronics, industrial equipment, telecommunications infrastructure, and automotive applications. BeiLuo provides technical support for power supply design, including component selection, PCB layout guidance, and thermal analysis.

selectionGuide.title: Power Management IC Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting optimal power management solutions

faqs.question: How do I choose between DC-DC converter and LDO?
faqs.answer: Choose DC-DC converter (buck) when: Input voltage is significantly higher than output (Vin > Vout + 1V), High current required (>500mA), Efficiency is critical (battery applications), Heat dissipation is a concern. Choose LDO when: Input and output voltages are close (Vin ≈ Vout), Low noise required (RF, analog circuits), Simple design preferred, Cost is primary concern. DC-DC offers 85-95% efficiency but with switching noise. LDO offers clean output but with efficiency = Vout/Vin.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use DC-DC for high efficiency with large voltage drops. Use LDO for low noise and simple designs.

faqs.question: What is the difference between synchronous and asynchronous DC-DC?
faqs.answer: Synchronous DC-DC uses MOSFET for both high-side and low-side switches. Advantages: Higher efficiency (2-5% better), especially at light loads. Lower voltage drop. Better for battery applications. Asynchronous DC-DC uses diode for low-side rectification. Advantages: Lower cost, simpler design, more robust. Synchronous is preferred for most modern designs except lowest-cost applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use synchronous for battery and efficiency-critical apps. Use asynchronous for lowest cost.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power dissipation and thermal requirements?
faqs.answer: Power dissipation calculation: For LDO: Pd = (Vin - Vout) × Iout. For DC-DC: Pd = Pout × (1/efficiency - 1). Temperature rise: ΔT = Pd × θja (thermal resistance). Junction temperature: Tj = Ta + ΔT. Keep Tj below 125°C for reliability. Thermal management options: Copper area on PCB, thermal vias, heatsinks, airflow. BeiLuo provides thermal simulation support for complex designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate worst-case dissipation. Ensure Tj < 125°C. Add margin for safety.

faqs.question: What input and output capacitor selection guidelines apply?
faqs.answer: Input capacitor: 10-22µF ceramic typically sufficient. Place close to IC input pin. Add 0.1µF for high frequency. Output capacitor: Affects stability and transient response. Follow datasheet recommendations exactly. Typical: 22-100µF with appropriate ESR. Ceramic preferred for low ESR. Tantalum or electrolytic for higher capacitance. Output ripple: Vripple = Iripple × ESR. Lower ESR = lower ripple.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow datasheet recommendations. Use ceramic for best performance. Consider ripple requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right current rating?
faqs.answer: Current rating selection: Calculate maximum load current. Add 20-30% margin for reliability. Consider startup and transient currents. Check thermal limitations at max current. Efficiency typically decreases at very light and very heavy loads. For pulsed loads, consider average vs peak current. BeiLuo FAEs can review load profiles and recommend optimal ratings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size for 120-130% of max load. Consider thermal limits. Check efficiency at operating points.

products.name: 3A Synchronous Buck Converter
products.shortDescription: High-efficiency 3A synchronous buck converter with 4.5-28V input and 95% peak efficiency for industrial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The USD3410 is a high-efficiency synchronous buck converter delivering up to 3A continuous output current.
With wide input voltage range of 4.5V to 28V and adjustable output from 0.8V to 24V, it serves diverse applications from industrial 24V systems to 12V consumer electronics.
Peak efficiency of 95% and low quiescent current make it ideal for both high-power and battery-powered applications.

faeReview.author: Zhang Wei
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Products
faeReview.content: The USD3410 is one of our most popular DC-DC converters. I've designed it into dozens of industrial and consumer products. The wide input range handles everything from 5V USB to 24V industrial supplies. Efficiency is excellent - consistently above 90% across most operating conditions. The adjustable frequency is useful for EMI optimization - you can trade off efficiency for smaller magnetics. Thermal performance is good with the exposed pad package. Price is very competitive compared to TI and MPS alternatives. For 3A buck applications, this is my first recommendation.

alternativeParts.reason: Established TI part with extensive design resources
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring minimum Vin < 4.5V or extensive reference designs

faqs.question: What is the efficiency curve for USD3410?
faqs.answer: USD3410 efficiency characteristics: Peak efficiency: 95% at 50-70% load, Vin=12V, Vout=5V. Light load efficiency: >80% at 10mA with PFM mode. Heavy load efficiency: >90% at 2-3A. Efficiency decreases with larger Vin-Vout differential. 12V to 3.3V: 92-94% typical. 24V to 5V: 90-93% typical. 24V to 3.3V: 88-91% typical. Efficiency improves with higher switching frequency but at cost of higher switching losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect 90%+ efficiency for most operating points. Higher efficiency with smaller voltage differential.

faqs.question: How do I set the output voltage?
faqs.answer: USD3410 output voltage set by resistor divider: Vout = 0.8V × (1 + R1/R2). Recommended R2 = 10kΩ. Calculate R1 = R2 × (Vout/0.8V - 1). Example: For 3.3V output: R1 = 10k × (3.3/0.8 - 1) = 31.25kΩ. Use 1% tolerance resistors for accuracy. Place divider close to FB pin. Add 10-22pF feedforward capacitor across R1 for improved transient response.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard resistor values. Place divider close to IC. Add feedforward cap for stability.

faqs.question: What inductor value should I use?
faqs.answer: Inductor selection for USD3410: Recommended range: 4.7µH to 22µH. Lower inductance: Smaller size, faster transient, higher ripple. Higher inductance: Lower ripple, better efficiency at light load, larger size. Typical values: 10µH for 12V to 5V at 3A. 15µH for 24V to 12V at 2A. 22µH for 24V to 5V at 3A. Current rating: Select inductor with saturation current > 1.3 × Iout_max. DCR: Lower is better for efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with 10-15µH. Adjust based on ripple and transient requirements.

faqs.question: How do I optimize EMI performance?
faqs.answer: USD3410 EMI optimization techniques: Switching frequency: Lower frequency reduces EMI but increases inductor size. Spread spectrum: Enable if available to spread energy. Input filtering: Add LC filter on input for conducted EMI. Output filtering: Ferrite bead on output for high frequency noise. Layout: Minimize switch node area. Shielding: Use ground plane under inductor. Snubber: Add RC snubber on switch node if needed. Testing: Use near-field probes to identify sources.
faqs.decisionGuide: Optimize layout first. Add filtering as needed. Test early and often.

faqs.question: Can USD3410 be used for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: USD3410 is not AEC-Q100 qualified for automotive. For automotive applications: Consider USD3410Q (automotive grade version). Temperature range: Industrial -40°C to +85°C. Automotive version: -40°C to +125°C. Load dump protection needed for 24V automotive systems. Additional filtering for automotive EMI requirements. Contact Unisplendour for automotive-qualified alternatives and support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use USD3410Q for automotive. Add protection for load dump and transients.

faqs.question: What protection features does USD3410 include?
faqs.answer: USD3410 protection features: Overcurrent protection: Cycle-by-cycle current limiting. Thermal shutdown: Shutdown at 160°C, hysteresis restart. Overvoltage protection: Output OVP latches off. Undervoltage lockout: Prevents operation below 4V input. Short circuit protection: Current limiting during short. Soft start: Reduces inrush current at startup. These protections make the device robust for industrial and consumer applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Built-in protections sufficient for most apps. External fuse recommended for safety-critical.

products.name: Low-Noise LDO Regulator
products.shortDescription: 300mA low-noise LDO with 1.8-5.5V input, ultra-low dropout, and 20µVRMS noise for RF and precision analog applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The USL1801 is a high-performance low-dropout (LDO) linear regulator optimized for noise-sensitive applications.
With ultra-low output noise of 20µVRMS and high power supply rejection ratio (PSRR) of 70dB at 1kHz, it provides clean power for RF circuits, ADCs, and precision analog systems.
The device features low dropout voltage of 150mV at 300mA and is stable with small ceramic capacitors.

faeReview.author: Li Hua
faeReview.title: FAE - Analog Products
faeReview.content: The USL1801 is my go-to LDO for noise-sensitive applications. I've used it in RF transceivers, precision measurement systems, and high-end audio products. The noise performance is excellent - 20µVRMS is comparable to much more expensive LDOs from ADI and TI. PSRR is also very good, effectively filtering switching noise from upstream DC-DC converters. Dropout voltage is reasonable at 150mV. The small SOT-23-5 package is convenient for space-constrained designs. For RF and precision analog applications where noise matters, this LDO delivers premium performance at a competitive price.
faeReview.highlight: Low-noise LDO with excellent PSRR for RF/analog

alternativeParts.reason: Lower noise for most demanding applications
Higher current, lower cost but much higher noise and dropout
alternativeParts.useCase: Cost-sensitive applications where noise is not critical

faqs.question: What makes USL1801 suitable for RF applications?
faqs.answer: USL1801 RF-suitable features: Ultra-low noise: 20µVRMS doesn't add phase noise to VCOs. High PSRR: 70dB at 1kHz filters switching noise. Clean output: No switching artifacts. Fast transient: Responds quickly to PA current steps. Low dropout: Can work with minimal headroom. These characteristics make it ideal for powering VCOs, PLLs, mixers, and LNAs in RF systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for VCO, PLL, mixer, LNA power. Cascade after DC-DC for efficiency.

faqs.question: How do I minimize output noise further?
faqs.answer: Techniques to reduce USL1801 output noise: Bypass capacitor: Add 0.01-0.1µF ceramic close to load. Filter: Add LC or RC filter for specific frequencies. Reference: Use external reference voltage if adjustable version. Layout: Keep traces short, minimize loop area. Input: Clean input supply with adequate bypassing. Load: Minimize load transients. These techniques can reduce effective noise below 10µVRMS at the load.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use bypass caps at load. Consider output filter for critical applications.

faqs.question: Can I use USL1801 for ADC reference voltage?
faqs.answer: USL1801 is excellent for ADC reference applications: Low noise: 20µVRMS preserves ADC SNR. Stable: No switching artifacts affecting conversion. Accurate: 1% initial accuracy, good for 12-bit+ ADCs. Clean: No ripple-induced code errors. Considerations: Use fixed voltage version for best accuracy. Add bypass cap at ADC reference pin. Ensure load regulation doesn't affect reference. For highest precision, consider dedicated voltage reference IC.
faqs.decisionGuide: Excellent for most ADC applications. Use dedicated reference for >16-bit precision.

faqs.question: What is the dropout behavior of USL1801?
faqs.answer: USL1801 dropout characteristics: Dropout voltage: 150mV at 300mA load. Scales with load current: ~50mV at 100mA, ~150mV at 300mA. Behavior in dropout: Output follows input minus dropout. Pass transistor fully on. No damage but regulation lost. Recovery: Returns to regulation when Vin > Vout + Vdropout. Thermal: May increase in dropout due to higher Vdrop × Iload. Design for Vin > Vout + 300mV to avoid dropout operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Maintain Vin > Vout + 300mV for proper regulation. Account for input ripple.

faqs.question: How does USL1801 handle load transients?
faqs.answer: USL1801 transient response: Response time: ~10µs for 10-90% of load step. Undershoot/overshoot: <5% for 50% load step. Output capacitor: Larger cap improves transient but slows response. Ceramic caps: Use low-ESR ceramic for best transient. Load step: Minimize di/dt if possible. For large load steps: Consider adding bulk capacitance at load. Or use DC-DC for high-current switching loads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Add output capacitance for large load steps. Consider DC-DC for switching loads.

faqs.question: What is the power dissipation in USL1801?
faqs.answer: USL1801 power dissipation: Pd = (Vin - Vout) × Iout. Example: 3.3V to 1.8V at 200mA = (3.3-1.8) × 0.2 = 300mW. Thermal considerations: SOT-23-5 thermal resistance: ~200°C/W. Temperature rise: 300mW × 200°C/W = 60°C. Max ambient: 85°C - 60°C = 25°C max. For higher power: Use larger package if available. Add copper area for heatsinking. Reduce Vin-Vout differential. Consider DC-DC for high current or large differentials.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate dissipation and temperature rise. Use DC-DC for high power.

categories.name: Industrial Control ICs
categories.description: Specialized integrated circuits for motor control, power metering, and industrial automation with high reliability and precision.
categories.longDescription: As an authorized Unisplendour distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive selection and support for industrial control ICs. Unisplendour's portfolio provides reliable solutions for motor control, power metering, and factory automation. The product line includes motor driver ICs for stepper, DC, and brushless motors, power metering chips with high accuracy for smart grid applications, and specialized control ICs for industrial equipment. These products are designed for harsh industrial environments with extended temperature ranges and robust protection features. BeiLuo provides application support including reference designs, motor tuning assistance, and system integration guidance.

selectionGuide.title: Industrial Control IC Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting motor drivers and control ICs for industrial applications

faqs.question: How do I select the right motor driver for my application?
faqs.answer: Motor driver selection depends on motor type and requirements: Stepper motors: Use stepper drivers with microstepping (USMD series). DC motors: Use H-bridge drivers with PWM control. Brushless DC: Use 3-phase drivers with sensorless or sensored commutation. Key parameters: Voltage rating > motor voltage, Current rating > motor current with 20% margin, Package thermal capability, Protection features (OCP, OTP, UVLO). Contact FAE for specific motor matching.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match driver to motor type. Size current for 120% of motor rating. Consider thermal requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between chopper and linear stepper drivers?
faqs.answer: Chopper (PWM) drivers: High efficiency, 70-90%, Cooler operation, Better for high current, More complex control, Audible noise possible. Linear drivers: Lower efficiency, 30-50%, Simple control, Smooth operation, No PWM noise, Good for low current precision. Chopper drivers preferred for most applications. Linear only for noise-sensitive or very low current applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use chopper drivers for most applications. Use linear for noise-sensitive low-current apps.

faqs.question: How accurate are Unisplendour power metering ICs?
faqs.answer: Unisplendour power metering IC accuracy: Class 0.2S: ±0.2% accuracy for revenue metering. Class 0.5S: ±0.5% accuracy for commercial metering. Class 1.0: ±1% accuracy for industrial monitoring. Accuracy maintained over: Wide temperature range (-40°C to +85°C), Wide voltage range (±20% of nominal), Wide current range (1% to 120% of nominal). Calibration can improve accuracy further.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Class 0.2S for billing. Class 0.5S for commercial. Class 1.0 for monitoring.

faqs.question: What protection features are important for industrial motor drivers?
faqs.answer: Essential motor driver protections: Overcurrent protection (OCP): Prevents driver damage from stall or short. Thermal shutdown (OTP): Protects from overheating. Undervoltage lockout (UVLO): Prevents erratic operation at low voltage. Overvoltage protection (OVP): Protects from regenerative spikes. Shoot-through protection: Prevents bridge short circuits. These protections ensure reliable operation in industrial environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure all basic protections present. Add external protection for critical applications.

faqs.question: Can Unisplendour industrial ICs operate in harsh environments?
faqs.answer: Unisplendour industrial ICs designed for harsh environments: Temperature range: -40°C to +85°C standard, -40°C to +125°C for extended range. Humidity: Operation up to 95% RH non-condensing. Vibration: Qualified to industrial vibration standards. ESD protection: >4kV HBM on all pins. EMI immunity: Designed for industrial electromagnetic environments. Package options: Industrial grade packages with enhanced reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify temperature range for application. Extended temp available for harsh environments.

products.name: Dual Stepper Motor Driver
products.shortDescription: Dual bipolar stepper motor driver with 2.2A peak current and 256 microstepping for CNC and 3D printer applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The USMD2208 is a high-performance dual stepper motor driver designed for precision motion control applications.
With 2.2A peak current per channel and up to 256x microstepping, it provides smooth, quiet operation for CNC machines, 3D printers, and robotic systems.
The integrated SPI interface allows real-time configuration of current, microstepping, and protection parameters.

faeReview.author: Chen Wei
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Motor Control
faeReview.content: The USMD2208 is an excellent stepper driver for precision motion applications. I've designed it into several CNC controllers and 3D printers. The 256x microstepping provides very smooth motion - much better than the 16x on older drivers. SpreadCycle chopper is significantly quieter than traditional constant off-time choppers. StallGuard2 is useful for sensorless homing and collision detection. The SPI interface makes configuration flexible - you can adjust current on the fly for power saving. Thermal performance is good with proper PCB heatsinking. Price is competitive with Trinamic alternatives. For dual-axis stepper applications, this is a great choice.
faeReview.highlight: High-performance dual stepper with advanced features

alternativeParts.reason: Single motor alternative with higher current

alternativeParts.useCase: Cost-sensitive applications not requiring advanced features

companionParts.description: Evaluation board with dual stepper motors

faqs.question: What is SpreadCycle chopper and why is it better?
faqs.answer: SpreadCycle is an advanced chopper control: Traditional: Constant off-time, audible noise at specific frequencies. SpreadCycle: Cycle-by-cycle current control, spreads energy across frequency spectrum, significantly quieter operation, better current regulation. Benefits: 10-20dB noise reduction, smoother motor operation, better torque at low speeds. Recommended for all applications where noise matters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use SpreadCycle for quiet operation. Enable in SPI configuration.

faqs.question: How does StallGuard2 work for sensorless homing?
faqs.answer: StallGuard2 load measurement: Measures back EMF during operation. Detects increased load when motor stalls. Provides digital load value via SPI. Can trigger interrupt on stall. Sensorless homing: Drive motor toward limit slowly. Monitor StallGuard value. Detect stall when hitting mechanical limit. Set position zero. Benefits: Eliminates limit switches, reduces wiring, improves reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use StallGuard for sensorless homing. Calibrate threshold for your mechanics.

faqs.question: What is CoolStep and how does it save power?
faqs.answer: CoolStep adaptive current control: Measures motor load via StallGuard. Reduces current when load is light. Increases current when load increases. Typical savings: 30-50% power reduction. Benefits: Cooler motor operation, extended motor life, reduced power consumption, quieter at light loads. Configure via SPI with load thresholds.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable CoolStep for variable load applications. Set appropriate thresholds.

faqs.question: How do I set the motor current?
faqs.answer: USMD2208 current setting: Via SPI: Write current value to register (0-31 scale). Via analog: External potentiometer to AREF pin. Calculation: Irms = (setting / 31) × 1.5A. Example: Setting 20 = (20/31) × 1.5 = 0.97A RMS. Peak current = Irms × 1.414. Start with lower current and increase gradually. Motor should run warm but not hot (<80°C).
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SPI for digital control. Start conservative and increase. Monitor motor temperature.

faqs.question: What PCB layout considerations are important?
faqs.answer: USMD2208 layout guidelines: Power ground: Large copper area for heat dissipation. Decoupling: 100µF electrolytic + 100nF ceramic near power pins. Current sense: Kelvin connection to sense resistors. Output: Short traces to motor connectors. Thermal: Via stitching under exposed pad. Keep digital signals away from motor outputs. Follow evaluation board layout for best results.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow reference layout. Use adequate copper for heatsinking. Keep loops small.

faqs.question: Can USMD2208 drive other motor types?
faqs.answer: USMD2208 motor compatibility: Bipolar stepper: Primary design, optimal performance. DC brushed: Possible with both coils in parallel, not recommended. Unipolar stepper: Convert to bipolar by ignoring center taps. Voice coil: Possible with appropriate current limiting. Stick to bipolar steppers for best results. For other motor types, use dedicated drivers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for bipolar steppers only. Use dedicated drivers for DC or BLDC motors.

products.name: Three-Phase Energy Metering IC
products.shortDescription: Class 0.2S three-phase energy metering IC with harmonic analysis and tamper detection for smart grid applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The USPM6530 is a high-accuracy three-phase energy metering IC designed for smart grid and industrial power monitoring applications.
With Class 0.2S accuracy and comprehensive harmonic analysis up to the 31st harmonic, it provides detailed power quality information for utility and industrial metering.
The integrated tamper detection and multiple communication interfaces make it ideal for modern AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure) deployments.

faeReview.author: Liu Ming
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The USPM6530 is a comprehensive metering solution for three-phase applications. I've supported deployments in smart meters, industrial monitoring, and EV charging systems. The 0.2S accuracy is genuine - we've verified against reference standards. Harmonic analysis is valuable for power quality monitoring in industrial settings. The tamper detection features are sophisticated - detecting magnetic tampering, reverse current, and neutral diversion. Communication interfaces are flexible - SPI for local MCU, UART for PLC or RF modules. Reference design includes complete analog front-end with CTs and PTs. For three-phase metering applications, this IC provides excellent value compared to ADE7758 or STPM32 alternatives.
faeReview.highlight: High-accuracy three-phase metering with power quality analysis

alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring maximum accuracy without harmonic analysis

companionParts.description: Complete meter reference design with CTs

faqs.question: What does Class 0.2S accuracy mean?
faqs.answer: Class 0.2S accuracy specification: 0.2% maximum error at reference conditions. 'S' indicates special accuracy at low currents. Accurate from 1% to 120% of rated current. Temperature coefficient: <0.01%/°C. Phase angle error: <0.1°. Suitable for revenue billing meters. Higher classes: 0.5S (commercial), 1.0 (industrial). Lower classes: 0.1S (premium, not available). USPM6530 meets international IEC and ANSI standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: 0.2S suitable for billing meters. Use 0.5S for commercial, 1.0 for monitoring only.

faqs.question: How does harmonic analysis work?
faqs.answer: USPM6530 harmonic analysis: Measures voltage and current waveforms. Performs FFT analysis. Calculates harmonics up to 31st order. Reports: Individual harmonic magnitude, Total Harmonic Distortion (THD), Power factor, Displacement vs true PF. Update rate: Once per second typical. Applications: Power quality monitoring, Harmonic filtering verification, Equipment diagnostics, Compliance checking.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable for power quality monitoring. Use THD to identify harmonic issues.

faqs.question: What tamper detection features are included?
faqs.answer: USPM6530 tamper detection: Magnetic tamper: Detects external magnetic fields. Reverse current: Detects current flow direction reversal. Neutral diversion: Detects imbalance between phases. Cover open: External switch input. Voltage imbalance: Phase loss detection. Event logging: Timestamp and type recorded. Alarm output: Interrupt to host MCU. These features help utilities detect and prevent electricity theft.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable all tamper detection features. Log events for investigation.

faqs.question: How do I calibrate the meter?
faqs.answer: USPM6530 calibration procedure: Gain calibration: Apply known voltage/current, adjust gain registers. Phase calibration: Adjust for CT phase shift. Offset calibration: Zero input should read zero. Temperature calibration: Optional for wide temperature range. Calibration equipment: Reference meter (0.05% or better), Stable voltage/current sources, Calibration software. Stored in EEPROM or MCU flash. Recalibration period: Typically 5-10 years.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calibrate at production. Use accurate reference equipment. Store calibration data securely.

faqs.question: What is the typical application circuit?
faqs.answer: USPM6530 typical application: Voltage sensing: Resistor dividers from each phase to neutral. Current sensing: Current transformers (CTs) on each phase. Anti-aliasing filters: RC filters before ADC inputs. Reference voltage: External 2.5V or 3.3V reference. MCU interface: SPI or UART connection. Power supply: 3.3V regulated from AC or external. Isolation: Required for safety - optocouplers on communication lines. Reference design includes complete schematic and BOM.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow reference design closely. Ensure proper isolation for safety.

faqs.question: Can USPM6530 be used for single-phase metering?
faqs.answer: USPM6530 for single-phase: Yes, can use one phase channel. Other channels can be disabled or used for: Split-phase measurement, Multiple single-phase loads, Future expansion. Cost: Overkill for simple single-phase. Better alternatives: USPM6510 (dedicated single-phase). Use USPM6530 when: Future three-phase expansion planned, Power quality analysis needed, Inventory consolidation desired.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use USPM6510 for single-phase only. Use USPM6530 if future expansion or analysis needed.
